title: 36 Hours in the Lower East Side
blurb: The neighborhood that keeps reinventing itself without forgetting where it came from.

places.name: Katz's Delicatessen
places.address: 205 E Houston St, New York, NY 10002
places.desc: The definitive New York deli. Get the pastrami on rye. Take a ticket, tip your carver. The Saturday lunch line is part of the experience.

places.name: Russ & Daughters
places.address: 179 E Houston St, New York, NY 10002
places.desc: The original appetizing shop. The Super Heeb is the move. The cafe on Orchard has more seats. Come early on weekends.

places.name: Metrograph
places.desc: Independent cinema showing arthouse films and retrospectives. The downstairs cafe stands alone. Book tickets ahead — weekends sell out.

places.name: Economy Candy
places.desc: Floor-to-ceiling candy in a tiny Orchard Street shop unchanged for decades. Great for gifting or nostalgia. The bulk bins are the whole point.

places.name: Ivan Ramen
places.desc: The LES outpost of Ivan Orkin's ramen shop. The shio ramen is delicate and complex. The rye noodles are worth ordering around. Go early or expect a wait.

places.name: Scarr's Pizza
places.desc: Fresh mozzarella, organic flour, a plain slice that tastes exactly like pizza should. Counter seating is the move. Expect a weekend line.

places.name: Tenement Museum
places.desc: Tours of restored tenement apartments documenting immigrant life from the 1860s onward. Book ahead — tours fill up. The neighborhood context makes every room hit harder.

places.name: McNally Jackson
places.desc: Best indie bookshop in the neighborhood. Staff picks are genuinely curated. The magazine section rewards slow browsing. Coffee bar in the back.
